content:
The following patch causes a build error for me:

diff --git a/samples/rust/rust_minimal.rs b/samples/rust/rust_minimal.rs
index 8eb9583571d72..4d627476297ff 100644
--- a/samples/rust/rust_minimal.rs
+++ b/samples/rust/rust_minimal.rs
@@ -24,6 +24,8 @@ struct RustMinimal {

 impl kernel::Module for RustMinimal {
     fn init(_module: &'static ThisModule) -> Result<Self> {
+        pr_info!("This does not compile: {}\n", 5u64 - 1);
+        pr_info!("This is fine: {}\n", (5u64 - 1));
         pr_info!("Rust minimal sample (init)\n");
         pr_info!("Am I built-in? {}\n", !cfg!(MODULE));
         pr_info!(

Is this know and intentional?

Error:

error[E0277]: `kernel::fmt::Adapter<u64>` doesn't implement `core::fmt::Display`
  --> /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/rnull/samples/rust/rust_minimal.rs:27:9
   |
27 |         pr_info!("This does not compile: {}\n", 5u64 - 1);
   |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^--^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   |         |                                |
   |         |                                required by this formatting parameter
   |         `kernel::fmt::Adapter<u64>` cannot be formatted with the default formatter
   |
   = help: the trait `core::fmt::Display` is not implemented for `kernel::fmt::Adapter<u64>`
   = note: in format strings you may be able to use `{:?}` (or {:#?} for pretty-print) instead
   = help: the trait `core::fmt::Display` is implemented for `kernel::fmt::Adapter<&T>`
   = note: this error originates in the macro `$crate::print_macro` which comes from the expansion of the macro `pr_info` (in Nightly builds, run with -Z macro-backtrace for more info)
Can you try f1db6538794f ("rust: fmt: fix formatting expressions")? It is in -next.
I don't see that commit in rust-next. It seems to point at v6.19-rc4.
Found it on list, and it fixes the issue.
Andreas Hindborg has marked this topic as resolved.

Andreas Hindborg said:

I don't see that commit in rust-next. It seems to point at v6.19-rc4.

By -next I meant linux-next
i.e. it is in rust-fixes that I will send to Linus soon.
Thanks for the tag, by the way (I will not rebase to add it, since the commit has been there for more than a week, but it is appreciated nonetheless)
